answer: The Eiffel Tower was completed in 1889. It was dedicated on March 31, 1889. Construction took two years, two months, and five days.

results.title: 130th anniversary focus on 1889 - The Eiffel Tower
results.snippet: In 2019, the Eiffel Tower celebrates its 130th birthday! Indeed, 1889 was the year of the Eiffel Tower was “born”. Construction of the Eiffel Tower began in 1887 and was finished in record time for a structure of its size – 2 years, 2 months and 5 days – and stood as veritable technical feat. Gustave Eiffel raised a French flag at the top of the Tower in the presence of the Director of the World’s Fair and some of his employees, and certain personalities from the Government and the City of Paris. On May 15, the World’s Fair began and the Tower opened to the public, without even one elevator yet in operation. A book for the 130th anniversary of the Eiffel Tower. A gorgeous book for the 130th anniversary of the Eiffel Tower. Watch the 130th anniversary light show of the Eiffel Tower. Eiffel Tower with logo 130 years. To celebrate the 130th anniversary of its opening to the public, the Eiffel Tower has put together a memorable program.

results.title: Date of construction of the Eiffel Tower - PARISCityVISION
results.snippet: March 31, 1889: Assembly of the top is completed. In all, the work took two years, two months and five days and the structure was christened on March 21, 1889.

results.title: Eiffel Tower opens in Paris | March 31, 1889 | HISTORY
results.snippet: # Eiffel Tower opens in Paris. On March 31, 1889, the Eiffel Tower is dedicated in Paris in a ceremony presided over by Gustave Eiffel, the tower’s designer, and attended by French Prime Minister Pierre Tirard, a handful of other dignitaries and 200 construction workers. Out of more than 100 designs submitted, the Centennial Committee chose Eiffel’s plan of an open-lattice wrought-iron tower that would reach almost 1,000 feet above Paris and be the world’s tallest man-made structure. Unperturbed, Eiffel completed his great tower under budget in just two years. The elevators were not completed by March 31, 1889, however, so Gustave Eiffel ascended the tower’s stairs with a few hardy companions and raised an enormous French tricolor on the structure’s flagpole. The Eiffel Tower remained the world’s tallest man-made structure until the completion of the Chrysler Building in New York in 1930.
